patch.value: atomic import: per-row resume/compensation tag + in-batch dup-key guard + SDK resolve hardening (2026.7.19-1)
Resume/compensation per-row-precise via csv-txrow marker; in-batch dup-key guard skips; resolveCommitWorkspaceTransaction try/catch + cached; 102 tests green; release:check clean.

value.author: pi-glm
value.text:
Fixes for PR #35 review findings (Greptile/Gemini/Sourcery):

1. Version bump 2026.7.19 -> 2026.7.19-1 (same-day re-release; feature commit after the 06:10 UTC daily release of 2026.7.19).
2. CORE CORRECTNESS: resume/compensation was matching created rows by title (applied.byTitle) and update rows by key (applied.byKey). This is wrong for CSVs with duplicate titles or duplicate keys: a resumed 2nd same-titled row would find the 1st row's item via byTitle and be wrongly skipped; two same new keys would both plan as create (keyIndex not updated during planning) -> duplicate items. Fixed by stamping a per-row ownership marker csv-txrow:<txId>#<rowIndex> (source of truth) alongside the batch marker csv-tx:<txId>. loadAppliedByTransaction now returns byRowIndex: Map<number,string> parsed from the per-row marker; byTitle/byKey dropped. inspect()/compensate()/tally all use byRowIndex.get(row.rowIndex). Duplicate titles/keys are now irrelevant to resume/compensation.
3. In-batch duplicate-KEY guard: during planning, when --key is set, track keys claimed by earlier planned CREATEs in this run (claimedKeys). A later row repeating a not-yet-existing key is SKIPPED with a clear per-row warning + result.skipped++ (simplest correct behavior; documented in README + code). Pre-existing-in-tracker keys still update.
4. Sourcery perf: rowFields(headers,row) was computed twice per row in the planning loop (parsed + keyValue). Now computed once and reused.
5. Sourcery bug_risk: resolveCommitWorkspaceTransaction dynamic import wrapped in try/catch; throws an actionable CommandError (USAGE) if import fails or commitWorkspaceTransaction is not a function. Resolved function cached in a module-level var so repeated --atomic calls don't re-import.
6. Export now strips csv-tx: and csv-txrow: provenance tags (previously only csv-key:/csv-source: were stripped) so round-trip exports stay clean.
7. 5 new tests added to test/atomic.test.ts (12 atomic tests total, 102 suite total).

patch.value: Resume/compensation per-row-precise; in-batch dup-key guard; SDK resolve hardened; 102 tests green; release:check clean.
All 6 fixes applied + 5 new tests. 102 tests pass, release:check clean.

Completed: all PR #35 review findings addressed. Per-row resume/compensation tag (csv-txrow), in-batch duplicate-key guard (skip), rowFields computed once, SDK resolve try/catch + cached, export strips csv-tx/csv-txrow. 102/102 tests green, release:check clean. Released as 2026.7.19-1.
Completed; all 6 PR #35 review fixes applied, 102 tests green, release:check clean.

value.author: claude-agent
value.text: Round-2 follow-up (Greptile P1 'Compensated rows block retry'): loadAppliedByTransaction now EXCLUDES closed items. compensateCreate rolls back via pm close (not delete), leaving the csv-txrow tag on the closed item; counting those as applied made a post-rollback retry skip the rolled-back rows and import only the tail. Now closed (compensated) items are ignored so a retry re-imports the full batch (crash-interrupted runs leave items OPEN, so genuine resume is unaffected). +1 regression test (103 total). Verified by orchestrator (Claude).

value.author: claude-agent
value.text: Round-3 Greptile P1 (closed-status rows re-import/duplicate): my round-2 'exclude closed items' fix broke resume for rows whose CSV status is closed/canceled (upsertCreate passes p.status → legitimately-closed item). Replaced status-based exclusion with MARKER-based: loadAppliedByTransaction matches any item carrying the csv-txrow marker regardless of status; compensateCreate now STRIPS the csv-tx/csv-txrow markers before closing a rolled-back row (best-effort, runs whether open or already-closed). So marker-presence=applied (resume idempotently, incl. closed-status rows), marker-absent=rolled-back (re-import). +1 regression test (104 total). Verified by orchestrator.
